events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Brian May was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th Birthday to the iconic Brian May! His guitar riffs with Queen are truly out of this world. What's your favourite Queen track featuring Brian's legendary Red Special?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Mick Jagger, lead singer and co-founder of The Rolling Stones, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Icon
angles.copy: Happy 83rd Birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger! Six decades of rock and still moving like Jagger. What's your ultimate Stones anthem?

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first music video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony wasn't lost on us then, and it isn't now! How did MTV change music for you?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring singles like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: A quarter-century ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the game. From Manchester to the world, what's your favourite track from this Britpop masterpiece?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began in 1969
events.blurb: The iconic Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a three-day festival of peace and music, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Three Days of Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years ago today, the legendary Woodstock Festival kicked off. An unforgettable moment in music history. If you could have been there, which artist would you have loved to see?

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: August 16th marks the anniversary of the passing of 'The King of Rock and Roll', Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: The King's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley today, a true icon whose music continues to inspire generations. His impact on rock and roll is immeasurable.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: September 2nd marks the anniversary of the passing of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar God Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix today. His revolutionary guitar work changed music forever. What's your favourite Hendrix track?

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic 'Abbey Road' album in the UK on September 26, 1969, and in the US on October 1, 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still one of the most famous in history! What's your favourite track from it?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, lead singer of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock and roll rebel with an unmistakable voice. What's your go-to Pretenders track?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Liam Gallagher, the distinctive frontman of Oasis and a successful solo artist, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Mancunian Icon
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation and still rockin'. What's your favourite Liam moment, solo or with Oasis?

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, from his album of the same name, which was released in the US on September 9, 1971, and in the UK on October 8, 1971.

angles.name: A Song for Peace
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon released 'Imagine'. A timeless anthem for peace and unity that still resonates today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music with its ability to hold 1,000 songs.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: 25 years ago, Apple launched the original iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. It changed how we listened to music forever! Did you have one?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 007, the ultimate secret agent. What's your favourite Bond film or theme song? Licence to thrill us with your choices!

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles, legendary singer-songwriter and peace activist, was born on October 9, 1940.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to inspire. What's your favourite Lennon song?
